summary: Package shadows the `node` binary with a stub that breaks builds and opens a promo URL
details: package.json declares `"bin": { "node": "./shim.js" }`, causing npm to symlink shim.js as `node` inside `node_modules/.bin/`. Because that directory is prepended to PATH for `npm run` scripts, any subsequent `node...` invocation in the consuming project's scripts runs the shim instead of the real Node.js runtime. shim.js does not invoke real node; it uses `child_process.execSync` with a `process.platform` branch to run `open` / `cmd /c start` / `xdg-open` against the hardcoded URL https://github.com/X3r0Day/ and then calls `process.exit(0)`. Effect on the installer: every `node` command in npm scripts becomes a no-op that exits 0, silently corrupting builds and tests that depend on node actually executing, while also forcing the installer's default browser to navigate to the author's GitHub profile on each invocation. Naming a `bin` entry `node` is not a legitimate use case — it hijacks runtime resolution inside the installer's project.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
